feat.description: A new feature, application implementation, or challenge solution

fix.description: A bugfix in an application, utility package, or assignment

refactor.description: Code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature

perf.description: A change that improves runtime performance or build speeds

docs.description: Documentation updates across READMEs, ADRs, or internal notes

style.description: Formatting, missing semi-colons, whitespace (no logic changes)

chore.description: Routine repository maintenance, tooling updates, or dependency bumps

build.description: Changes affecting Turborepo pipelines, pnpm workspaces, or compilers

ci.description: Changes to GitHub Actions workflows or PR validation scripts

revert.description: Reverts a previous commit history entry

scopes.repo: Global repository tooling, root configurations, and workspace setup
scopes.ci: GitHub Actions workflows and automation pipelines
scopes.tooling: Shared configurations like tsconfig, eslint, or build scripts
scopes.apps: Any work inside the runnable applications directory (e.g., capstone, next-dashboard, online-shop)
scopes.packages: Any work inside the topic-focused learning workspaces (e.g., ts-advanced, nestjs, real-time)
scopes.challenges: Coding challenges, coding assignments, and leveling exercises across all 16 weeks

BREAKING CHANGE.description: The commit introduces architectural breaking changes

Closes.description: Closes a tracking issue or pull request

Co-authored-by.description: Credits mentors or cohort peers during pairing sessions
